proposed_hypotheses.text: The overall objective_response rate in the cohort is between 0.20 and 0.30 (a typical advanced-prostate-cancer ORR range), and feature_030 — which has only one observed value (0) — carries no usable variation for response prediction.

analyses.code: df['objective_response'].mean(); df['feature_030'].nunique()
analyses.result_summary: Overall ORR = 0.2402 (12,011 / 50,000 patients with objective_response = 1). feature_030 has a single value (0) for all 50,000 patients and was therefore excluded from all subsequent regressions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, at least one (likely a therapy indicator) is associated with a higher objective_response rate, and several others are associated with a lower rate (likely adverse clinical factors).
feature_008 = 1 is associated with a higher objective_response rate than feature_008 = 0, consistent with an active anti-cancer therapy.
feature_013 = 1 is associated with a substantially lower objective_response rate than feature_013 = 0 (likely a marker of more aggressive or visceral disease).

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_008')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: ORR with feature_008 = 1 is 0.3614 (n=20,076) vs 0.1589 with feature_008 = 0 (n=29,924). Difference = +0.2024 percentage-point units. Chi-square test p < 1e-300.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_013')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: ORR with feature_013 = 1 is 0.1535 (n=27,481) vs 0.3460 with feature_013 = 0 (n=22,519). Difference = -0.1925. Chi-square p < 1e-300.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: chi-square ORR(c=1) vs ORR(c=0)
analyses.result_summary: Other binary features with significant ORR differences: feature_021=1 ORR 0.150 vs 0.250 (diff -0.100); feature_015=1 ORR 0.160 vs 0.260 (diff -0.100); feature_027=1 ORR 0.176 vs 0.242 (diff -0.066). Features with no significant main effect: feature_006, feature_005, feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004 (|diff| < 0.005).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 is the highly right-skewed lab variable (range 0.12 to 3622, mean 42.7) most consistent with PSA, and higher feature_022 is associated with lower objective_response.
feature_002 (range 1.5 to 5.5; consistent with serum albumin in g/dL) is positively associated with objective_response.
feature_020 (range 0 to 24.6) is negatively associated with objective_response, consistent with a disease-burden or pain marker.
feature_010 (integer 6 to 10; consistent with Gleason score) shows a negative monotonic association with objective_response: higher Gleason → lower ORR.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df.objective_response==1,'feature_022'], df.loc[df.objective_response==0,'feature_022'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_022 in responders = 28.2, in non-responders = 47.2. Welch t-test p = 1.46e-137. Sign of effect (responders − non-responders) is negative, supporting that higher feature_022 predicts lower response.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df.objective_response==1,'feature_002'], df.loc[df.objective_response==0,'feature_002'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_002 in responders = 3.820 vs 3.796 in non-responders. Welch t-test p = 3.6e-6. Direction confirms higher feature_002 → higher ORR.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df.objective_response==1,'feature_020'], df.loc[df.objective_response==0,'feature_020'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_020 in responders = 3.50 vs 3.95 in non-responders. Welch t-test p = 6.8e-31. Higher feature_020 → lower ORR.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_010')['objective_response'].mean(); ttest_ind by responder status
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_010 (Gleason-like) does NOT differ by response (responders 7.645 vs non-responders 7.640, p = 0.61). The expected monotonic association is absent in this cohort. Hypothesis h8 is REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-level ordinal, values 0/1/2 with frequencies 35%/50%/15%; consistent with ECOG performance status) shows monotonically decreasing ORR with higher category.
feature_024 (highly skewed, range 0.05 to 300, mean 6.1; consistent with AST or ALT) is negatively associated with objective_response after log-transformation.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_001')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_001: 0=0.282 (n=17,592), 1=0.228 (n=24,971), 2=0.182 (n=7,437). Strict monotonic decline. Chi-square across 3 levels p < 1e-50. Effect estimate = ORR(2) − ORR(0) = -0.100.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(np.log1p(feature_024) by responder)
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_024 in responders = 5.69 vs 6.23 in non-responders. Welch t-test p = 9.3e-9. Direction negative as predicted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic regression, the strongest independent predictors of objective_response are feature_008 (positive), feature_013 (negative), feature_015 (negative), feature_021 (negative), feature_027 (negative), feature_001 (negative ordinal), and log(feature_022) (negative).

analyses.code: sm.Logit(objective_response, all features incl. log(feature_022) and log(feature_024))
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression (n=50,000, pseudo-R^2 = 0.125). Significant coefficients (z>3 in absolute value): feature_008 +1.20 (z=52.7); feature_013 -1.07 (z=-38.6); feature_021 -0.74 (z=-16.9); feature_015 -0.69 (z=-22.1); feature_027 -0.43 (z=-5.9); feature_001 -0.33 per category (z=-19.3); feature_002 +0.13 (z=5.6); feature_020 -0.04 per unit (z=-12.1); log(feature_022) -0.08 (z=-7.1); log(feature_024) -0.09 (z=-6.4). All other binary candidate-treatment features (feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004) and feature_006/feature_005 had z-values < 1.5 — no independent main effect. feature_010 (Gleason-like) coefficient ~0 (z=0.03).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 on objective_response is heterogeneous across feature_013: feature_008=1 substantially raises ORR when feature_013=0 but not when feature_013=1 (negative T×M interaction).

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ T + M + T*M) with T=feature_008, M=feature_013; also stratified ORR
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_008 × feature_013 coefficient = -1.978 (logit scale), p ≈ 0 (z = -38). Stratified ORRs: feature_013=0: ORR(T=1)=0.6096 vs ORR(T=0)=0.1693 (Δ = +0.4403); feature_013=1: ORR(T=1)=0.1581 vs ORR(T=0)=0.1505 (Δ = +0.0077). The treatment is highly effective only when feature_013=0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_013=0 stratum, three additional binary clinical factors (feature_021, feature_015, feature_027) each act as INDEPENDENT suppressors of the feature_008 treatment effect: in patients with any of these set to 1, the treatment effect is abolished.

analyses.code: Within df[feature_013==0], for each m in [021,015,027]: Logit(y ~ T + m + T*m); also report cell ORRs
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013=0 (n=22,519): T×feature_021 interaction beta = -2.34, p = 1.3e-78 (cells: T=1,M=1: ORR=0.145; T=1,M=0: ORR=0.661; T=0 cells ~0.16). T×feature_015 interaction beta = -2.41, p = 4.5e-163 (T=1,M=1: 0.177; T=1,M=0: 0.717). T×feature_027 interaction beta = -2.17, p = 2.6e-27 (T=1,M=1: 0.192; T=1,M=0: 0.622). All three nominally negative, very large effects. Each suppresses the T effect to ~0 when present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full subgroup definition for an effective feature_008 treatment effect on objective_response is: feature_013=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_027=0. Inside this subgroup, the average treatment effect is roughly +0.6 in absolute ORR; outside it is ~0.

analyses.code: fav = (013==0)&(021==0)&(015==0)&(027==0); compare ORR within fav and within ~fav by treatment; Logit(y ~ T + fav + T*fav)
analyses.result_summary: Favorable subgroup size N=15,681 (31.4% of cohort). Within favorable: ORR(feature_008=1)=0.7978 (n=6,325) vs ORR(feature_008=0)=0.1721 (n=9,356), Δ = +0.6257 ppt (chi-square p = 0). Within unfavorable (n=34,319): ORR(T=1)=0.1606 vs ORR(T=0)=0.1530, Δ = +0.0077 (n.s.). Logit interaction TF coef = +2.886 (z = 56.1, p ≈ 0); LPM TF coef = +0.618 (t = 84.8, p ≈ 0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four suppressors (feature_013, feature_021, feature_015, feature_027) is independently necessary in the subgroup definition: dropping the constraint on any one of them and toggling that variable to 1 collapses the treatment effect from ~+0.63 to ~0.

analyses.code: For each suppressor s, hold the other three at 0 and split by s∈{0,1}; compute ΔORR(T) in each cell.
analyses.result_summary: With other three suppressors = 0: feature_013=0 → ΔORR = +0.6257; feature_013=1 → ΔORR = +0.0074. feature_021=0 → +0.6257; feature_021=1 → +0.0038. feature_015=0 → +0.6257; feature_015=1 → +0.0215. feature_027=0 → +0.6257; feature_027=1 → -0.0247. Each suppressor's =1 value independently abolishes the treatment effect. All four are required for the subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup (all four suppressors = 0), no other feature meaningfully modifies the feature_008 treatment effect (no clinically large additional interaction).

analyses.code: Within fav, for each remaining feature m: Logit(y ~ T + m + T*m); inspect interaction p-values and effect sizes.
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup, screening of all remaining features yielded only weak interactions: feature_005 beta_int = -0.246 (p = 0.004); feature_017 beta_int = -0.285 (p = 0.033). None of the continuous lab features (incl. log(feature_022), feature_002, feature_020, feature_010, feature_026, feature_028 etc.) showed an interaction with p < 0.05. Even the two nominally significant binary interactions correspond to small differences in stratified ORR (e.g., within fav with feature_005=1, ORR(T=1)=0.788; with feature_005=0, ORR(T=1)=0.823). The four-suppressor subgroup is therefore the dominant treatment-effect modifier; no further refinement materially changes the conclusion. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A model-free, non-parametric subgroup discovery (T-learner gradient-boosted models, then a regression tree on individual treatment-effect predictions) will independently recover feature_013, feature_015, feature_021 as the top splits, supporting the four-suppressor subgroup definition.

analyses.code: GradientBoostingClassifier on T=0 and T=1; ITE = p1 - p0; DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=4, min_samples_leaf=500).fit(X, ITE)
analyses.result_summary: T-learner ITE summary: mean ITE in favorable subgroup = +0.611; in unfavorable = +0.014. Top splits in tree: feature_013, then feature_015, then feature_021, with ITE in the all-zero leaf ≈ 0.54-0.60 and ~0 elsewhere. Confirms the four-suppressor subgroup (feature_027 not picked because of its 3% prevalence and min_samples_leaf=500, but earlier targeted analyses show it is also a suppressor).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The five other binary features that look like candidate therapies (feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004) have no detectable treatment effect on objective_response — neither in the overall cohort nor in any patient subgroup defined by the other features.

analyses.code: For each candidate t: main-effect chi-square; t × m interaction screen for all binary, ordinal, and continuous m; subgroup ORR within favorable subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Main effects: |ORR(t=1) − ORR(t=0)| ≤ 0.005 for all five (none significant). Treatment × modifier interaction screens: NO interactions with p < 0.01 for any of the five candidates. Within the favorable subgroup (n=15,681), |ORR(t=1) − ORR(t=0)| ≤ 0.017 for all five with no p < 0.05. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — these five binary features have no measurable effect on objective_response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (prevalence 20%) and feature_005 (prevalence 60%) have negligible main effects on objective_response and do not modify the feature_008 treatment effect at clinically meaningful magnitudes.

analyses.code: df.groupby([c,'feature_008'])['objective_response'].mean() for c in [feature_006, feature_005]; logistic interaction tests.
analyses.result_summary: feature_006: ORR(=1)=0.2395 vs ORR(=0)=0.2404 (diff -0.0008, n.s.); no T×feature_006 interaction at p < 0.01. feature_005: ORR(=1)=0.2386 vs ORR(=0)=0.2426 (diff -0.0040); within favorable subgroup, weak T×feature_005 interaction (beta -0.24, p 0.004) but the absolute ORR(T=1) only drops from 0.823 (feature_005=0) to 0.788 (feature_005=1) — small magnitude. SUPPORTED that these are not high-impact effect modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-category, ECOG-like) is a prognostic factor for objective_response that operates ADDITIVELY with the four-suppressor subgroup: even within the favorable subgroup the response gradient by feature_001 is preserved.

analyses.code: Within favorable subgroup, df.groupby('feature_001')['objective_response'].mean(); also within unfavorable.
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup: feature_001=0 ORR=0.475 (n=5,511), =1 ORR=0.410 (n=7,824), =2 ORR=0.355 (n=2,346) — gradient preserved with magnitude similar to overall. T×feature_001 interaction within favorable not significant (beta -0.11, p=0.07). feature_001 acts as an additive prognostic factor, NOT as a treatment effect modifier. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher log(feature_022) (PSA-like marker) is independently associated with lower objective_response within the favorable subgroup as well as overall, but does NOT modify the feature_008 treatment effect.
Higher feature_020 (disease-burden-like marker) is independently associated with lower objective_response and does not modify the feature_008 treatment effect.

analyses.code: Within fav: Logit(y ~ T + log_022 + T*log_022); inside fav and overall.
analyses.result_summary: Overall multivariable model: log(feature_022) coef = -0.082 (z=-7.06, p=1.7e-12). Within favorable: T × log(feature_022) interaction coef -0.047, p = 0.34 — no significant modification. Main-effect direction holds. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Multivariable model and within-fav interaction test.
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable coef on feature_020 = -0.0365 per unit (z = -12.1, p = 1.0e-33). Within favorable: T × feature_020 interaction coef -0.0004, p = 0.97. Main-effect direction holds; no modification. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 (age-like, mean 65 years, range 30-90) does not predict objective_response after multivariable adjustment, suggesting age is not a primary determinant of response in this cohort.
feature_018 (range 48-830, mean 224; consistent with a lipid or alkaline-phosphatase analyte) does not have an independent association with objective_response after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Univariate t-test and multivariable Logit coefficient on feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate: mean feature_016 by response = 65.01 vs 64.98 (p = 0.82). Multivariable: coef = 0.0004 (z = 0.34, p = 0.73). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Multivariable Logit coefficient on feature_018.
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable coef = -6.3e-5 per unit (z = -0.45, p = 0.65). No independent association. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_010 (integer 6-10; Gleason-score-like) is uncorrelated with objective_response in this cohort, contrary to the typical clinical prior that higher Gleason carries worse prognosis.

Renal/electrolyte features (feature_007 sodium-like, feature_014 potassium-like, feature_032 calcium-like, feature_025 creatinine-like, feature_012 bilirubin-like) have no independent association with objective_response after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Univariate ttest and Logit coef on feature_010.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate p = 0.61 (mean 7.65 vs 7.64 by response). Multivariable coef = 0.0003 (z = 0.03, p = 0.98). Hypothesis SUPPORTED — Gleason-like feature is not informative here.

analyses.code: Multivariable Logit coefficients.
analyses.result_summary: All five renal/electrolyte/liver features have multivariable z < 2 in absolute value (sodium z=-0.44, potassium z=-1.74, calcium z=-0.40, creatinine z=-0.37, bilirubin z=-1.30). No independent associations at the conventional 0.05 threshold. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Hemoglobin-like feature_026 (range 6-18, mean 12.5) does not have an independent association with objective_response after multivariable adjustment in this cohort.

analyses.code: Multivariable Logit coef on feature_026.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate diff in means by response = -0.012 (p=0.51). Multivariable coef = -0.0022 per unit (z = -0.35, p = 0.73). Hypothesis SUPPORTED — feature_026 is not a primary independent predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined definition of the favorable subgroup: the four-suppressor rule (feature_013=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_027=0) yields a treatment-effect estimate of approximately +63 percentage points for feature_008 — the absolute response increase 0.798 − 0.172 = 0.626.

analyses.code: Within fav: ORR(T=1) - ORR(T=0); chi-square; LPM coefficient on TF.
analyses.result_summary: Within four-suppressor-favorable subgroup (n=15,681): ORR(T=1)=0.7978 (n=6,325) vs ORR(T=0)=0.1721 (n=9,356), Δ = +0.6257 ppt. Chi-square p = 0. LPM interaction term TF = +0.618 (t = 84.8). Logit interaction term coef = +2.886 (p ≈ 0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the unfavorable subgroup (any of feature_013, feature_021, feature_015, feature_027 = 1), no detectable treatment effect of feature_008 remains after adjustment for prognostic covariates.

analyses.code: Within unfav: Logit(y ~ T + covariates).
analyses.result_summary: Within unfavorable subgroup (n=34,319): unadjusted ΔORR(T) = +0.0077 (n.s.). After adjustment for feature_001, feature_002, feature_020, log(feature_022), log(feature_024), feature_013, feature_021, feature_015, feature_027 (still 0/1 within stratum): adjusted feature_008 coef = +0.062 (z = 2.04, p = 0.041) on the logit scale, corresponding to roughly +0.01 absolute ORR — clinically negligible compared with the +0.63 effect inside the favorable subgroup. The qualitative conclusion (no meaningful effect outside favorable subgroup) is SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four suppressors (feature_013, feature_021, feature_015, feature_027) themselves act as INDEPENDENT prognostic factors (each lowers ORR among feature_008=0 patients), in addition to abolishing the treatment effect of feature_008.

analyses.code: Within control arm (feature_008=0): chi-square ORR(s=1) vs ORR(s=0) for each suppressor.
analyses.result_summary: In feature_008=0 (n=29,924): feature_013=1 ORR=0.151 vs =0 ORR=0.169 (Δ -0.018, p=4e-5); feature_021=1 ORR=0.145 vs 0.160 (Δ -0.015, p=0.02); feature_015=1 ORR=0.156 vs 0.159 (Δ -0.003, n.s.); feature_027=1 ORR=0.184 vs 0.158 (Δ +0.026, n.s.). Suppressors have only modest direct prognostic effects; their dominant role in the data is to suppress the treatment benefit, not to drive baseline ORR. Hypothesis only PARTIALLY SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pairwise interactions among candidate-treatment features (feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004) and feature_008 are absent: there is no synergistic combination effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: For each pair (t1,t2): Logit(y ~ t1 + t2 + t1*t2); also test triple interactions T(008) × t × m.
analyses.result_summary: All pairwise interactions among {feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004} on objective_response: max |coef| = 0.13, min p = 0.06. None survive multiple testing. Triple interactions feature_008 × t × m within favorable subgroup: also none significant at p<0.01. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — no synergistic combinations among these candidate treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The proportion of patients in whom feature_008 is administered (40.2%) is essentially the same in the favorable subgroup (40.3%) and the unfavorable subgroup (40.1%), indicating no strong selection bias in who receives the treatment with respect to the four suppressors.

analyses.code: df.groupby(fav)['feature_008'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Pr(feature_008=1 | favorable) = 6,325/15,681 = 0.4033. Pr(feature_008=1 | unfavorable) = 13,751/34,319 = 0.4007. Chi-square p = 0.59. Treatment assignment is essentially uniform across the suppressor-defined subgroups, so the heterogeneous treatment effect is not an artifact of differential selection. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL CONSOLIDATED HYPOTHESIS: The treatment effect of feature_008 = 1 on objective_response is concentrated entirely in the subgroup defined by feature_013 = 0 AND feature_015 = 0 AND feature_021 = 0 AND feature_027 = 0; within this subgroup the absolute increase in ORR from feature_008 is approximately +0.63 (from 0.17 to 0.80), and outside this subgroup the effect is essentially zero (~+0.01). All four suppressor variables are individually necessary — toggling any one to 1 removes the treatment benefit.

analyses.code: fav = (013==0)&(021==0)&(015==0)&(027==0); compare ORR(T=1) vs ORR(T=0) within fav and ~fav; logistic interaction; per-suppressor leave-one-out check.
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup (N=15,681, 31.4% of cohort): ORR(feature_008=1) = 0.7978 vs ORR(feature_008=0) = 0.1721; Δ = +0.6257 (chi-square p = 0). Within unfavorable (N=34,319): Δ = +0.0077 (n.s.). Logistic interaction (T*F) coef = +2.886 (z = 56.1, p ≈ 0). Linear-probability interaction = +0.618 (t = 84.8). Each suppressor independently necessary (leave-one-out drops Δ from ~+0.63 to <+0.025 when the dropped suppressor flips to 1). Hypothesis fully SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sensitivity check: the +0.63 ppt subgroup-restricted treatment effect estimate for feature_008 is robust to adjustment for the strong continuous prognostic features (log(feature_022), feature_020, feature_002) and the ordinal feature_001; that is, the treatment effect is not confounded by these covariates within the favorable subgroup.

analyses.code: Within fav: Logit(y ~ T + feature_001 + feature_002 + feature_020 + log_022 + log_024); coefficient on T.
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup, multivariable Logit yields coef on feature_008 = +3.021 (z = 70.2, p ≈ 0). On the LPM scale, adjusted Δ = +0.625 (t = 100.0). The treatment effect is preserved with full prognostic adjustment for feature_001, feature_002, feature_020, log(feature_022), log(feature_024), confirming it is not confounded by these covariates. SUPPORTED.
